text_blocks.text: eventually 354 MW SEGS CSP installation, in
the Mojave Desert of California, is the
largest solar power plant in the world. Other
large CSP plants include the 150 MW Solnova
Solar Power Station and the 100 MW Andasol
solar power station, both in Spain. The 250
MW Agua Caliente Solar Project, in the United
States, and the 221 MW Charanka Solar Park
in India, are the world's largest photovoltaic
plants. Solar projects exceeding I GW are
being developed, but most of the deployed
photovoltaics are in small rooftop arrays of
less than 5 kW, which are grid connected using
net metering and/or a feed-in tariff. In 2013
solar generated less than 1% of the worlds
